method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/acs (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace + embedded Google Maps JS) and traced its public actions to their real homes: reporting child abuse links out to the NY State OCFS Statewide Central Register (ocfs.ny.gov, 1-800-342-3720) and service/provider complaints to NYC 311 (portal.311.nyc.gov). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Administration for Children's Services (ACS)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 21 assets with column schemas (16 are non-tabular file attachments, only 5 tabular, 1 address-level).

meta.platform: NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' platform (Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace + Google Maps JS); no ACS-owned application — public actions delegated to NY State OCFS (Central Register) and NYC 311
meta.netnew: ChildCareComplaint — submit a complaint about a child care provider (models the provider concern, never case data; abuse routed to the State Central Register)
meta.tagline: A confidentiality-bound agency whose only machine-readable asset is a provider directory and whose two public actions are delegated to the State and to 311.
meta.headline: ACS has no hidden backend to unlock — its data is confidential or dumped as static files, and its abuse and provider-complaint intakes belong to the NY State Central Register and NYC 311, so the fruit is to insource an owned contract for what ACS can publish and should own.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace); no ACS-specific application at all.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No ACS content or site API; informational HTML only.
scorecard_notes.openData: 21 assets but 16 are non-tabular file attachments; only 5 tabular and just 1 address-level.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Operational data confidential by statute — published only as aggregate reports, mostly as files, never per case.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No ACS-owned write surface; abuse reports go to the NY State Central Register, provider complaints to NYC 311.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no structured intake, only downloadable spreadsheets and delegated web forms.
scorecard_notes.ownership: ACS owns the Community Partners directory but delegates both public intakes; ownership of the transaction layer is absent.

headline_findings: ACS is a confidentiality-bound agency: abuse/neglect investigations, foster care, and juvenile detention are confidential by statute and published only in aggregate — never as individual cases.
Its Open Data footprint is thin and mostly non-tabular: of 21 ACS assets, 16 are 'file' attachments (uploaded Excel/PDF reports you cannot query) and only 5 are tabular datasets.
Exactly one ACS dataset is address-level and machine-readable — ACS Community Partners (9hyh-zkx9, 39 columns), the provider directory. Everything else is aggregate accountability reporting.

Its two public actions are delegated, not owned: reporting child abuse goes to the NY State OCFS Statewide Central Register (1-800-342-3720), and complaining about a child care provider goes to NYC 311 (portal.311.nyc.gov).

Net-new write surface: submit a child-care-provider complaint (ChildCareComplaint) as an owned ACS contract instead of deferring to 311 — modeling the provider concern, never case data, and redirecting any suspected abuse to the State Central Register. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 21 ACS assets, but only 5 tabular and 1 address-level (Community Partners 9hyh-zkx9); the rest are non-queryable file attachments. The one useful open ACS API.
Where reporting child abuse/neglect actually goes; not an ACS system and deliberately human-mediated, not machine-readable.
Where child care / provider complaints and other ACS-adjacent requests are filed; ACS defers to 311 rather than owning an intake.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM, embedded Google Maps JS. No content API.
Client-side program/locator maps on child-care and child-welfare pages.

fruit.name: ACS Community Partners (provider directory)
fruit.notes: The single address-level, machine-readable ACS entity: program name, operating agency, address, weekly office hours, contact, and full NYC geography spine (BBL/BIN, council district, community board, NTA, lat/long).

fruit.name: Child Welfare Indicators
opendata_match: Report to City Council on Demographics uhvm-6sct (13c)
fruit.notes: Aggregate counts of SCR intakes, investigations (indicated/unsubstantiated), emergency removals, Article X filings, and referrals to prevention — by period and geography. No individual case, ever.

fruit.name: Preventive Services
opendata_match: Children Served in Preventive Services ding-39n6 (file)
fruit.notes: Children/families served, new cases, utilization, and case-closure outcomes rolled up by borough and community district. Aggregate only; never a per-family record.

fruit.name: Foster Care statistics
opendata_match: Foster Care Placements by CD xg3x-h3g7 (file)
fruit.notes: Children in 24-hour care, placements, permanency outcomes, graduation rates, educational continuity, government ID, and psychiatric-medication use — aggregate by period/geography. No individual child.

fruit.name: Youth & Family Justice statistics
opendata_match: Report to City Council on Preplacement iwat-y983 (9c)
fruit.notes: Detention admissions, placement demographics, incident reports, and pre-placement figures — aggregate by period and geography. No individual youth.

fruit.name: Report child abuse or neglect
fruit.notes: Delegated to the NY State OCFS Statewide Central Register (1-800-342-3720) — a staffed, phone-based system, not an ACS API and deliberately not an anonymous machine surface. Listed as a delegated action, by design; NOT the net-new write surface.

fruit.name: Complain about a child care provider
fruit.notes: A complaint about a child care PROVIDER (health/safety/staffing/licensing/quality) is filed through NYC 311, not an ACS-owned intake. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain: an owned ChildCareComplaint contract that models the provider concern, never case data, and redirects suspected abuse to the State Central Register.

fruit.name: Individual case / child record
fruit.notes: Individual investigations, foster children, and youth are never published — confidential by statute (Social Services Law) and held in internal ACS/CCWIS systems. Correctly no public surface; the API must preserve this.

fruit.name: Monthly Flash Report indicators (Jan 2015 - Dec 2025)
fruit.notes: ACS Monthly Flash Report child-welfare indicators as a single .xlsx (11 years); the website serves these only as monthly PDFs.

fruit.name: Outcome of Preventive Cases Closed CY2014 by Borough and CD
fruit.notes: Preventive-services case-closure outcomes by borough and community district (CY2014).
